hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: MySQL has built-in functions for reading files from the server filesystem. If the database user has FILE privilege, these functions work.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: LOAD_FILE('/path/to/file') reads a file and returns its contents as a string. You can use this inside a UNION SELECT to display the file contents.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: First determine the number of columns in the query. Then use UNION SELECT with LOAD_FILE() to read the flag file. The flag is at /var/flag.txt.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: The query has 4 columns. Try: 0 UNION SELECT LOAD_FILE('/var/flag.txt'),2,3,4-- to read the flag file and display it in the first column.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: Navigate to: /patient?id=0 UNION SELECT LOAD_FILE('/var/flag.txt'),2,3,4-- The LOAD_FILE function reads /var/flag.txt from the MySQL server's filesystem and returns it as the patient name field. The flag is displayed in the patient details.
